skill.name: edit-article
skill.description: Edit and improve articles by restructuring sections, improving clarity, and tightening prose. Use when user wants to edit, revise, or improve an article draft.
skill.summary: Improve article drafts through clearer structure, tighter prose, and section-level revision.
skill.author: mattpocock

security_audit.summary: The static weak cryptographic algorithm finding at SKILL.md:3 is a false positive. The line is frontmatter description text for an article editing skill, and no evidence found of cryptography, code execution, network access, filesystem access, or prompt injection.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Static Finding: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged SKILL.md:3, but the line is a natural language description of article editing behavior. No evidence found of weak cryptographic algorithm usage or related executable code. Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. Confidence: 0.97. Confidence reasoning: The file contains only Markdown instructions, and line 3 has no cryptographic API, algorithm selection, or data processing logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged line is plain frontmatter text for an editing skill. There is no code path where cryptography could execute.

content.value_statement: Long drafts often lose readers through weak structure and unclear section flow. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code revise articles with clearer dependencies, shorter paragraphs, and better coherence.
actual_capabilities: Divides an article draft into sections based on headings.
Evaluates the order of sections using information dependencies.
Asks the user to confirm the section plan before rewriting.
Rewrites each section for clarity, coherence, and flow.
Keeps rewritten paragraphs within a 240 character maximum.
limitations: Does not fact-check claims or verify citations.
Does not define a target publication style guide.
Requires headings or clear section boundaries for best results.
Covers article editing only, not image, layout, or publishing tasks.

use_cases.title: Revise a technical article
use_cases.description: Improve the order, clarity, and paragraph flow of a technical draft before publication.

use_cases.title: Prepare an internal explainer
use_cases.description: Turn a rough internal document into a clearer article with logical section progression.

use_cases.title: Polish a newsletter essay
use_cases.description: Tighten long sections and improve reader flow while preserving the main argument.

prompt_templates.title: Improve a draft
prompt_templates.prompt: Please edit this article draft. First divide it into sections, then ask me to confirm the section plan before rewriting.

prompt_templates.title: Clarify section order
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this article for section order and dependency flow. Suggest a better structure, then wait for my approval before editing.

prompt_templates.title: Tighten prose by section
prompt_templates.prompt: Rewrite each approved section for clarity and coherence. Keep paragraphs under 240 characters and preserve the original meaning.

prompt_templates.title: Run a publication edit
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this draft for structure, information dependencies, clarity, and paragraph length. Confirm the plan with me before producing the revised article.

output_examples.input: A long article draft with headings, mixed argument order, and dense paragraphs.
output_examples.output: A confirmed section outline followed by rewritten sections with clearer flow and shorter paragraphs.

output_examples.input: A technical explainer that introduces advanced concepts before required background.
output_examples.output: A reordered article plan that places prerequisite ideas first, then a cleaner section-by-section rewrite.

output_examples.input: A newsletter essay with strong ideas but uneven transitions.
output_examples.output: A revised essay with smoother transitions, clearer section purpose, and concise paragraph structure.

best_practices: Provide the full draft with headings so the skill can identify sections accurately.
Review and approve the proposed section plan before requesting rewritten sections.
State the intended audience and publication context before the rewrite begins.
anti_patterns: Do not ask it to verify facts, citations, or legal claims without separate source material.
Do not use it for documents that need strict brand or regulatory wording without review.
Do not skip the section confirmation step when the article structure is important.

faq.question: What does this skill edit?
faq.answer: It edits article drafts by improving section structure, clarity, coherence, flow, and paragraph length.

faq.question: Does it rewrite the whole article at once?
faq.answer: No. It first divides the article into sections and asks for confirmation before rewriting each section.

faq.question: Can it improve technical writing?
faq.answer: Yes. It is useful for technical articles when the draft has headings and clear subject matter.

faq.question: Does it check whether claims are true?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on editing and structure. Provide sources if factual verification is required.

faq.question: Why does it limit paragraph length?
faq.answer: The skill instructs rewrites to use paragraphs of no more than 240 characters.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
